dimensions.rationale: All twenty-six frozen S2 requirements are explicit, required and reciprocally projected exactly once into required workstreams, acceptance criteria and thirty-six evidence artifacts; final severity and product-budget rules are measurable and fail closed.

Core lifecycle semantics, five thin skills, isolated surface adapters, change-set providers, authority profiles and release tooling have explicit one-way boundaries, failure behavior, compatibility seams and rollback rules.

The canonical fifteen-workstream DAG is acyclic, exact recursive write sets are disjoint, shared mutable state is controller-owned, reviewers are independent and workers contain no duplicate dependsOn authority.
Required adapter work is fully offline, pinned to the immutable contract inventory and capped honestly at EXPERIMENTAL; optional live promotion cannot block or weaken the required release DAG.

Bootstrap, per-launch, post-attempt and release neutrality scopes are fail-closed, externally trusted, signed, subject-bound and redacted, with explicit secret, permission and deny-authority boundaries.

The frozen product profile defines exact S0/S1/S2 logical-call, token, context, tool, evidence, parallelism and wall-time ceilings plus deterministic p50/p95 benchmark and bounded live-calibration gates.

Every requirement has reciprocal acceptance and bounded evidence with a producer command, receipt, validator identity, runtime class, threshold, freshness and reuse rule; task and final acceptance reject every open Medium, High or Blocker finding.

Offline adapter execution is non-blocking, while externally owned neutrality, live-calibration and exact nine-leg CI authorities have explicit launch gates, signed receipts, finite timeouts and fail-closed aggregation.

Specification and review lineage, the unavailable-subject disclosure, strict revision increment, approval modes, bounded retries, durable state, dependency acceptance, controller gates and no-open-Medium verdict rules are explicit; both ready-profile validators pass revision 3.

The provider-neutral task-local plan retains eight effective waves, width four, zero overlaps, zero duplicate validation commands and a 52565-byte maximum task context under the 65536-byte ceiling; deterministic scripts precede semantic work.

findings.message: The plan provides the complete bootstrap negative suite, full repository scan, signed controller-owned pre-launch and post-attempt neutrality gates for WS-02 through WS-15, explicit external trust inputs, full-history and recursive release-payload evidence, and final verification of signer, subject, scope, freshness and zero-result counters.
findings.requiredAction: No further action for PLAN-002; preserve the frozen neutrality authority profile, controller receipt bindings, full bootstrap/release scopes and redaction boundary through freeze and implementation.

findings.message: Frozen specification revision 3, reciprocal manifest projections and every task/final gate require no unresolved Medium, High or Blocker finding for ACCEPTED or COMPLETE, with Blocker stopping execution immediately.
findings.requiredAction: No further action for PLAN-003; retain the exact task and final severity thresholds through freeze and implementation.

findings.message: All fifteen forbidden worker.dependsOn members are absent. Workstreams remain the sole canonical DAG, their dependencies are unchanged and acyclic, the quality analyzer still reports eight effective waves and width four, and both manifest-ready and full plan-ready validation pass on plan revision 3.
findings.requiredAction: No further action for PLAN-007; keep dependencies exclusively in workstreams[].dependsOn and reject any future worker-level DAG duplication.

findings.message: The exact first specification-review subject is preserved as product-specification-r01-round01.snapshot.json, specification-review-r02 links specification-review-r01 by exact content identity, and the append-only r03/r04 chain reaches the valid specification-freeze-r03 receipt. The unavailable rejected plan-r01 subject remains separately disclosed, while this review links the exact r02 artifact that itself links r01.
findings.requiredAction: No further action for PLAN-001; preserve the specification chain, plan-review-r01 and r02 artifacts and plan-review-r01-subject-disclosure.json unchanged.

findings.message: WS-10 through WS-13 run with network denied and no live-surface capability gate, consume the immutable normalized contract inventory, pin closed offline compatibility descriptors and emit release-eligible EXPERIMENTAL evidence; VERIFIED remains an optional separately authorized live promotion.
findings.requiredAction: No further action for PLAN-004; keep live surface capability outside the required DAG and prevent offline evidence from promoting maturity above EXPERIMENTAL.

findings.message: The immutable product budget profile freezes S0/S1/S2 hard ceilings, stage token limits, logical-call and cumulative-context semantics, five benchmark scenarios, two hundred deterministic cold/warm runs, absolute p95 SLOs, later-release p50/p95 regression limits, signed baseline sequencing and six bounded live calibration runs.
findings.requiredAction: No further action for PLAN-005; preserve the exact profile, benchmark authority, reciprocal EV-RESOURCE and EV-COST-BENCHMARK contracts, and distinction between implementation-run and shipped-product limits.

findings.message: The plan freezes the exact Ubuntu, macOS and Windows by CPython 3.11 through 3.13 matrix, nine distinct CI-owned evidence and receipt paths, an external signed allowlist authority, exact per-leg environment identities and a signed fail-closed aggregate consumed by release evidence.
findings.requiredAction: No further action for PLAN-006; preserve all nine leg identities, per-leg receipts, external authority and exact aggregate consumption through freeze and implementation.

summary: READY_TO_FREEZE after a final independent full review of all ten dimensions for DRAFT plan revision 3. The corrected authoritative subject is bound to e14c0396640df66e3d663ac48bac1f932e45306eb0e8af856e8194264d55bb49 and the exact r02 predecessor review is bound to 1a8802de1b96a6fbe4dc69372b95cb67b04d09a1986a58de6ac7e9e628c87e5b. Deterministic manifest-ready and plan-ready validation pass; all worker-level dependsOn fields are absent; workstreams remain the unchanged canonical DAG; and the analyzer reports fifteen tasks, eight effective waves, width four, zero write overlaps, zero open questions, zero duplicate commands, thirty-six evidence artifacts and a 52565-byte maximum task context below the 65536-byte ceiling. PLAN-001 through PLAN-007 are resolved, every dimension passes and no open Blocker, High or Medium finding remains. The package is ready for fail-closed plan freeze against this exact subject; it is not yet frozen or authorized for implementation.
optimizationNotes: The r02 correction and required revision increment preserve the safe execution shape: critical path 8, effective waves 8, maximum width 4, zero write overlaps, zero open questions and zero duplicate validation commands.
Total referenced context is 421371 bytes while maximum task context remains 52565 bytes under the 65536-byte ceiling; no context or DAG reduction is justified before freeze.
Keep workstream dependencies as the sole DAG authority. Reintroducing worker.dependsOn would add no execution information and would make the ready profile fail again.

Retain the single controller-owned content-addressed neutrality gate and referenced authority profile; duplicating its contract or external values into task packets would increase context and weaken redaction.

The final safe next step is deterministic freeze of this exact revision-3 reviewed hash. Any change to scope, ownership, DAG, acceptance, evidence or authority profiles requires a higher revision and independent review rather than an in-place lock edit.
